---
title: "Standard Errors in Practice"
description: "Robust, cluster-robust, and HAC inference, with guidance for applied work."
---

## Motivation {#sec-standard-errors-motivation}

*This chapter is under development.* It will explain why default standard errors often fail in real datasets and how to choose robust, cluster-robust, and HAC alternatives.

### What You Will Learn

- When homoskedastic standard errors are invalid
- How HC, cluster, and HAC estimators differ
- How standard-error choice changes t-tests and confidence intervals
- Practical reporting rules and common inference mistakes

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Coming Soon

This chapter will include:

- **Intuition** — Why “same coefficients” can imply different conclusions.
- **Formalization** — Sandwich variance estimators for HC, cluster, and HAC.
- **Proofs** — Asymptotic validity conditions and failure cases (collapsed by default).
- **Interactive code** — Compare CI width and rejection rates across SE choices.
:::
